2606.31552.title: Improving multichannel speech enhancement through accurate room-acoustic simulations
2606.31552.paper_summary: Room-acoustic simulations are widely used to augment training data for deep-learning-based speech enhancement. While most pipelines rely on simplified geometrical acoustics, wave-based approaches offer greater physical accuracy. In this work, we examine how simulation fidelity affects multichannel speech enhancement performance. To this end, we train SpatialNet on datasets augmented with different room-acoustic simulation methods and evaluate the resulting models on measured data. We compare lower-fidelity datasets based on geometrical acoustics with a high-fidelity dataset using advanced acoustic modelling and a hybrid combination of wave-based and geometrical acoustics simulations. Training on the high-fidelity dataset results in an up to 38 % relative reduction in median word error rate compared to the lower-fidelity alternatives. These results show that augmentation with high-fidelity room-acoustic simulations directly translates into improved multichannel speech enhancement performance.
2606.31552.paper_summary_zh: 房間聲學模擬被廣泛用於增強基於深度學習的語音增強訓練數據。雖然大多數流程依賴於簡化的幾何聲學，但基於波的方式提供了更高的物理準確性。在這項工作中，我們檢查模擬的真實性如何影響多通道語音增強的性能。為此，我們在不同房間聲學模擬方法增強的數據集上訓練SpatialNet，並在測量數據上評估所得到的模型。我們將基於幾何聲學的低真實性數據集與使用先進聲學建模的高真實性數據集以及波基和幾何聲學模擬的混合組合進行比較。在高真實性數據集上訓練的結果顯示，與低真實性替代方案相比，中位數單詞錯誤率最多減少38%。這些結果顯示，使用高真實性房間聲學模擬的增強直接轉化為多通道語音增強性能的改善。
2606.31552.author: Georg Götz et.al.
2606.31552.authors: Georg Götz, Alessia Milo, Steinar Guðjónsson, Daniel Gert Nielsen, Jesper Pedersen, Finnur Pind
